title: [$250] RBR and stale Home to-do persist after deleting a broken card feed

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

labels.name: Hot Pick
labels.description: Ready for an engineer to pick up and run with

body:
## Bug Description After removing a third-party (Plaid) card feed that has a broken connection, stale data remains in Onyx causing two visible issues: 1. **Company cards page shows RBR (Red Brick Road) on the empty state** — the card feed was successfully deleted, but the error indicator persists as if there's still something wrong. 2. **Home page shows a to-do to fix a broken bank connection** — this bank connection no longer exists since the feed was removed, so the to-do is confusing and incorrect. ## Reproduction Steps 1. Have a workspace with a third-party (Plaid) card feed 2. Let the card feed connection break (e.g., the Plaid connection expires and needs re-authentication) 3. As a copilot, remove/delete the card feed entirely 4. Observe the Company cards page — it shows the empty state but still has RBR 5. Observe the Home page — it shows a to-do to fix a broken bank connection that no longer exists ## Expected Behavior After deleting a card feed: - The Company cards page should show a clean empty state with no RBR - The Home page should not show a to-do for a bank connection that was deleted ## Actual Behavior - Company cards empty state still shows RBR - Home page shows a stale to-do to fix a broken bank connection ## Additional Context - The card feed was deleted as a copilot, which may be relevant to the issue - Affected email: jason@expensifail.com - It appears that Onyx is not properly cleaned up when a card feed is removed, leaving behind stale error/connection state **Slack thread:** https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1775729373571389
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @sobitneupane

title: [$250] Optimize getTransactionsSections and getReportSections

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Coming from https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1775743804085649 ### Background SearchUIUtils.ts powers the Search feature by transforming raw API data into renderable list items. Before processing individual results, the two main functions (getTransactionsSections and getReportSections) need to derive several pieces of metadata -- key classification, column visibility flags, violation maps, and action lookup tables -- each computed by a separate helper that iterates over the entire Object.keys(data). ### Problem The data object is traversed 7+ times by independent helpers (getShouldShowMerchant, buildLastExportedActionByReportIDMap, shouldShowYear, getWideAmountIndicators, getViolations, createReportActionsLookupMaps) before real work begins, and report-level violation checks (hasAnyViolations + hasVisibleViolationsForUser) double-iterate the same violations per report row. This redundant work is a measurable bottleneck on large result sets. ### Solution A new classifyAndPreprocess(data) function replaces all 6 helpers with a single pass that produces every derived structure at once, and a new computeViolationFlags() merges the two violation checks into one loop with early exit. Performance gains | Function                             | Previous Avg (ms) | Current Avg (ms) | Δ (ms) | Improvement | |-------------------------------|----------------------|---------------------|---------|-----------------| | getReportSections            | ~10.9                     | ~8.55                    | -2.35   | ~21.6%          | | getTransactionsSections   | ~0.96                     | ~0.75                    | -0.21   | ~21.9%         | Branch and scenario in the :thread:
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @dmkt9

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
I was recently doing some research and testing with the sign-up experience, and I came across this issue: - User recently signs-up - User creates an expense - We show a `Spend over time` graph with exactly one data point. Here's an example from a test account: Given that we have exactly one type of `Spend over time` graph on Home, which is grouped by month, I **think our solution is simply to not show `Spend over time` on Home until a user minimally has two months of data**. cc @puneetlath @shawnborton since we talked about this one. Also assigning to the original project team!

Open FAB > Create expense > Manual 2. Enter an amount and press Enter 3. On the confirm page, verify the Back button does NOT have a blue focus ring 4. Press Tab ## Expected Result: Focus moves to the first interactive element (e.g To field) on the confirm page (not the Back button) ## Actual Result: It first focusing to the back button, then to the next interactive element (e.g To field) ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4fdf3a62-2995-459d-9182-cc4b49b64ee2 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.55-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87034 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** ibellicotest+2250@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Pull Request QA execution **Device used:** Motorola MotoG60 / Android 12 (Hybrid app) **App Component:** Notifications ## Action Performed: Prerequisite: Require two accounts in a policy, a submitter and a manager. (User A as submitter and User B as manager/owner) 1. Open the Expensify app as User A. 2. Open workspace chat where User B is manager. 3. Create a manual expense. 4. Open report of the just created expense and send a message. 5. Navigate away from report to "Inbox" 6. As User B, open the expense created by User A. 7. Tap on "Description" field, add a description and save. 8. As User A, note that a notification is received showing that manager just edited the description of the expense. ## Expected Result: Notifications shouldn´t be received for non-actionable actions on a report, such as the edition of description. ## Actual Result: Submitter of a workspace receives a notification after the manager edits the description of an expense. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14fb86f7-0e91-4b9e-8637-28c6fa54a550 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Update the table and row styles for Mobile Reports page

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: NewFeature
labels.description: Something to build that is a new item.

body:
We are in the process of updating the row/table styles for web. We also want to make similar changes for Mobile as well. The various list views on the Reports page suffer from a few problems: - They are inconsistent with Classic (Expenses list does not show status badge) - The font size for supporting elements is very tiny (11px) - They are visually very busy and hard to digest We’d like to propose some improvements to clean up the visual style and make these lists feel lighter, more modern, and easier to parse. Here’s what we’re thinking: - Bring status badges to the Expenses list - Bump small font sizes up to 13px - Remove action buttons and tags from rows to reduce visual clutter - Create a consistent template that can be used across the various row types found on Reports The result is this (Figma is [here](https://www.figma.com/design/DMwzhMmaL09kc8PayTEPun/NewDot-Compact-Styles?node-id=769-306165)): Individual mocks for easier inspectability: cc @Expensify/design @JmillsExpensify @trjExpensify @JS00001 @luacmartins
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @situchan
